title: Fresh Dense Portuguese Cucumber Pastry
description: Indulge in this refreshing Portuguese pastry featuring fresh cucumber and rich score-fat, perfect for a dairy-free-friendly breakfast or snack.
instructions: Combine non-dairy milk, eggs, sugar, and yeast in a large mixing bowl.
Add flour, salt, and vegan butter to the bowl, mixing until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and roll it out to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
Spread score-fat evenly over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Layer sliced cucumbers on top of the score-fat, overlapping them slightly.
Roll the dough into a tight log, starting from the long side.
Place the log seam-side down on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Cut the log into 1-inch thick slices, cutting at an angle to create a decorative edge.
Place the pastries on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving 1 inch of space between each pastry.
Brush the tops with non-dairy milk and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve warm, dust with powdered sugar if desired.
Serve with a dollop of non-dairy whipped cream if desired.
